expectations.text: The code uses vLLM as the serving engine
expectations.evidence: solution.py line 61 installs 'vllm==0.9.2' and line 102 launches 'vllm serve' via subprocess.Popen.

expectations.text: The code uses @modal.web_server to expose the HTTP endpoint
expectations.evidence: solution.py line 99: '@modal.web_server(port=PORT, startup_timeout=10 * MINUTES)' decorates the serve() function.

expectations.text: The endpoint is OpenAI-compatible (serves /v1/chat/completions or equivalent)
expectations.evidence: vLLM's built-in OpenAI-compatible server is used. The smoke-test client (line 177) POSTs to '{base_url}/v1/chat/completions', confirming the expected route.

expectations.text: The code pins a specific model revision (e.g., a commit SHA or tag) when pulling from HuggingFace
expectations.evidence: solution.py line 37: MODEL_REVISION = '5206a32e0bd3067aef1ce90f5528ade7d866253f' (a full commit SHA), passed to vLLM via '--revision MODEL_REVISION' at line 106.

expectations.text: The code stores model weights in a Modal Volume to avoid re-downloading on cold starts
expectations.evidence: solution.py lines 72-78: 'hf_cache = modal.Volume.from_name("llama-3-1-8b-huggingface-cache", create_if_missing=True)' mounted at '/root/.cache/huggingface' (line 91). A second volume for vLLM compile artifacts is also included.

expectations.text: The code includes a readiness check, health check, or smoke-test client
expectations.evidence: Two mechanisms present: (1) lines 124-136 poll 'http://0.0.0.0:{PORT}/health' in a loop until the server is healthy before returning from serve(); (2) lines 144-203 define a @app.local_entrypoint() smoke test that checks /health and sends a chat-completions request.

expectations.text: The code sets HF_XET_HIGH_PERFORMANCE=1 or equivalent to speed up Hub downloads
expectations.evidence: solution.py line 62: '.env({"HF_XET_HIGH_PERFORMANCE": "1"})' is chained onto the image definition.

claims.claim: Model is meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct pinned to revision 5206a32e0bd3067aef1ce90f5528ade7d866253f
claims.evidence: solution.py lines 34 and 37 confirm the model name and revision SHA.

claims.claim: GPU is A10G (24 GB VRAM), appropriate for an 8B BF16 model
claims.evidence: solution.py line 40: GPU = 'a10g'. Transcript Step 5 explains the VRAM rationale.

claims.claim: HF_XET_HIGH_PERFORMANCE=1 is set to speed up Hub downloads
claims.evidence: solution.py line 62 sets the env var in the image definition.
solution.py lines 49 and 115-116: FAST_BOOT read from environment, appends '--enforce-eager' when truthy.

claims.claim: Two separate Volumes are used — one for HF weights, one for vLLM compile artifacts
claims.evidence: solution.py lines 72-79: hf_cache and vllm_cache are distinct Volume objects mounted at separate paths.

claims.claim: Smoke test uses only the standard library (urllib), no extra dependencies
claims.evidence: local_entrypoint uses urllib.request and json exclusively — both stdlib modules.

suggestions.assertion: The code stores model weights in a Modal Volume to avoid re-downloading on cold starts
suggestions.reason: The assertion passes as long as a Volume is mounted at the HuggingFace cache path. It does not verify that the vLLM compile-artifact cache is also persisted, which the skill guide explicitly requires and the solution correctly implements. A separate assertion for the vLLM cache Volume would catch any implementation that omits it.

No assertion checks that the solution actually builds from a CUDA base image (nvidia/cuda) rather than a plain Python image. Cold-start correctness and GPU compatibility depend on this, and a solution that uses a non-CUDA image would pass all current assertions while likely failing at runtime.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions are meaningful and well-targeted. Two gaps worth addressing: the vLLM compile-artifact Volume is unasserted, and the CUDA base image requirement is unasserted. Everything else is covered with good specificity.
